mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising month-over-month, equity valuations expand due to low real rates and forward earnings expectations remain anchored, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: low-rate inflation regime with monetary accommodation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any rolling 12-month window post-trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate below 4.0% signals tight labor markets and wage pressure, which equity markets interpret as earnings headwinds and cost inflation, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of periods when UNRATE < 4.0%, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1500 thousand units, signaling weakening consumer demand and forward economic contraction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth < 0.2% AND HOUST < 1500 thousand units
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances, or the correlation between the composite signal and forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth remains above 0.3% month-over-month consistently, indicating robust supply-side capacity and productivity gains that support profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.01 over any rolling 12-month window, or SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 52% of high-INDPRO-growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100 thousand new jobs per month, signaling labor market softening and reduced consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 62% of instances when PAYEMS growth < 100k, or Rank IC remains above -0.01 over any rolling 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 0.25% while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains below 2.0% annualized, the Fed is tightening into economic weakness, creating a stagflation signal that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal's Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rise above 1700 thousand units while retail sales (RSAFS) accelerate above 0.5% month-over-month, indicating robust consumer and construction demand that supports equity earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1700 thousand units AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the composite signal falls below 0.02 over any rolling 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) decelerates to below 0.1% month-over-month growth while unemployment (UNRATE) rises above 4.2%, signaling supply weakness combined with labor softening that constrains earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal's Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any rolling 12-month window.
